---
title: "Programming Practice: Lab 9"
execute:
  enabled: true
  echo: false
  cache: true
format:
  html:
    code-fold: false
jupyter: python3
diagram:
  cache: true
  cache-dir: ./cache
  engine:
    tikz:
      execpath: lualatex
filters:
  - diagram
---

# Intro

OpenCV is a library for image manipulation. We will use its Python bindings. First, complete the following materials:

1. Enroll into OpenCV course (<https://opencv.org/university/free-opencv-course/>)
2. Complete 4 initial lessons:
   - Getting Started with Images
   - Basic Image Manipulation
   - Image Annotation
   - Image Enhancement
3. Read the following two articles on filtering using OpenCV:
   - <https://colab.research.google.com/drive/1bKnHP0ALcR0uL3OyyC0R55tJycfk5Lbh?usp=sharing#scrollTo=8DaCsYeNfAMj>
   - <https://learnopencv.com/image-filtering-using-convolution-in-opencv/>

# Exercise

1. Write Python code that will blur/sharpen an input image.
